title: Solenostemon koualensis
intro: Solenostemon koualensis, also known as Coleus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, headache, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Solenostemon koualensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Solenostemon koualensis include tea infusions and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments, as well as being used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
taxonomy: Solenostemon koualensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family.
